folio.label: 39v
folio.l: ¢ The Reve answered , and seide stynt thy clap~
Late be thyn lewed , dronken harlotrie
It is a synne , and eke a gret folie
To a peyren any man , of his defame
And eke to brynge wyues , in swicú name
Thow maist I now , of othere thynges seyn
¢ This dronken Millere , spak ful sone a geyn
And seide , leue brother Oswold
Who hath no wyf , he is no Cokewold
But I seye nat æäfore , that æè art on
There ben ful goode wyues , many on
Why art æè angry , with my tale now
I haue a wyf parde , as wel as thow
ñet nolde I , for the Oxen in my plougú
Taken vp on me , more than I nougú
As demen onö my self , æ I were one
I wol byleue wel , æat I am none
An husbonde , shal nat ben inquesitif´

note.notetxt: 53 In the left margin ¢ Nota bene. Abbreviation for 'nota bene' occurs in the right margin as well.ES
53There is a smal dot in brown ink next to the not ain the right margin.ODR

folio.l: Of goddes preuyte , ne of his wyf

note.notetxt: 54 A mark, possibly a paraph, is in the left margin.ES
An54The mark seems to point to the an husbonde. Same as in the former folio.ODR

folio.l: So he may fynden , goddes foyson there
Of the remennt , nedeth nat enquere
¢ What shulde I more seyn , but æis Millere
He nolde hise wordes , for noman for bere
But told his Cherles tale , in his manere
Me a thynketh , æ I shal , reherce it here
And therfore , euäy gentil wrygútwygútwrygút I pray
Demeth nat , for goddes loue , that I say
Of euele entent , but for I mot reherce
Here tales alle , al be they bet , or werce
Or elles falsen , somö of my matere
And æäfore , wo so list it nat , to here
Turne ouä the lef , and chese a noæä tale
For he shal fynde I nowe , grete & smale
Of storial thyng´ , that toucheth gentilnesse
And eke moralite , and holynesse
Blameth nat me , if æat ©e chese a mys
The Millere is a charl , ©e knowe wel this
So was the Reve eke , and othere moo
And harlotrie they tolden , bothe two
A vyseth ©ow , and put me ou©t of blame
And eke , men shulnö nat make , ernest of game

note.notetxt: 76There is a gap, which could have accommodated at least four lines before the lower ruling line of the frame.The scribe filled it with a long decorated descender from the r in ernest. The tale of the Miller begins immediately on the recto of the following 40rODR
